title: Runes vs legacy reactivity
description: Svelte 5 runes ($state/$derived/$effect) make reactivity explicit and deep, replacing implicit $: labels and assignment tracking.
body: Use when reading or migrating Svelte reactive code. Legacy Svelte tracks top-level assignments and uses $: labels for computed values and reactive statements, but cannot see in-place mutation of arrays/objects. Runes make it explicit: $state creates a deeply reactive proxy (so mutation works), $derived declares lazily recomputed values, and $effect runs side effects with cleanup. Do not mix the two models carelessly in one component or assume $: works inside runes mode. Actions: identify which mode a file is in and follow its rules; convert per component when migrating. Validation signals include tests proving updates fire under the chosen model. Risk boundary: mixed-mode reactivity produces subtle stale-UI bugs; treat a half-migrated component as a defect to finish or revert.
